channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: USAID workers will be given 15 minutes to clear their workspaces as the agency gets dismantled
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Thousands of U.S. Agency for International Development workers who have been fired or placed on leave as part of the Trump administration's dismantling of the agency are being given a brief window Thursday and Friday to clear out their workspaces.
item.dc:creator: GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chief Justice John Roberts pauses judge’s order for Trump admin to pay foreign aid contractors by midnight
item.description: U.S. Supreme Court Justice John Roberts has paused a federal judge’s order that required the Trump administration to pay out foreign aid funds to contractors by midnight.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Musk says federal workers should expect another round of ‘pulse check’ emails
item.description: President Trump warned federal workers who did not reply to recent emails asking them to describe “five things” they accomplished are “on the bubble” suggesting they are at risk of losing their jobs.
item.dc:creator: Heidi Glenn, MPR News

item.title: NIH partially lifts freeze on funding process for medical research
item.description: Thousands of grant applications had been stalled when the Trump administration blocked the National Institutes of Health from posting notices to the Federal Register.
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein, MPR News

item.title: Justice Department abandoning cases alleging discriminatory police and firefighter hiring
item.description: The Justice Department is abandoning Biden administration cases that sought to force police and fire departments to end alleged discriminatory hiring practices, Attorney General Pam Bondi said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pentagon orders new purge of social media sites to dump diversity, inclusion mentions by March 5
item.description: Building lethality in the military may be the buzzword for the new Trump administration, but busywork and paperwork have become the reality at the Pentagon, as service members and civilian workers are facing a broad mandate to purge all of the department’s social media sites and untangle confusing p
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor And Tara Copp, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Minneapolis, St. Paul join lawsuit against president over immigration enforcement
item.description: The White House argues St. Paul’s 2004 separation ordinance interferes with federal immigration enforcements, but St. Paul Mayor Melvin Carter said the city’s policy does not oppose lawful immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Chandra Colvin, MPR News

item.title: VA pauses billions in cuts lauded by Musk as lawmakers and veterans decry loss of critical care
item.description: The Department of Veterans Affairs has temporarily suspended billions of dollars in contract cuts after an uproar that critical veterans health services were harmed, lawmakers and veterans service organizations said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp And Carla K. Johnson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Lawmakers differ over location of Florida’s Black history museum
item.description: Florida has a rich and long history involving contributions from its Black residents. There are areas across the state highlighting those struggles and efforts. Some of those key moments are displayed at the Robert W Saunders, Sr Public Library in Tampa.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dem bid to end Trump energy emergency squashed after forced Senate vote
item.description: Democrats in the Senate failed to pass a resolution that would have reversed President Donald Trump’s executive order on "Unleashing American Energy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tampa Bay area families travel to Capitol Hill to urge lawmakers for more pediatric cancer funding
item.description: Embracing photos and wearing the names of their children who were taken too soon, families took off from Tampa International Airport Wednesday morning, headed for D.C. to urge lawmakers to pass more funding for pediatric cancer research.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Marino, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump’s social media video garners pushback from Arabs and Muslims in U.S. and Gaza
item.description: In a seemingly AI-generated video that the president posted on social media, images of destruction due to the war in Gaza are transformed into a glitzy resort called “TRUMP GAZA.
item.dc:creator: Sarah McCammon, Hadeel Al-Shalchi, and Anas Baba, MPR News

item.title: House unanimously passes bill to quadruple the pensions of Medal of Honor recipients
item.description: Legislation to quadruple pensions for Medal of Honor heroes passed the House Wednesday with strong bipartisan support and now goes to the United States Senate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nevada Gov. Lombardo, AG Ford clash on immigration policies ahead of potential 2026 matchup
item.description: Republican Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo and Democratic Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford -- who could face off in the 2026 gubernatorial election -- swapped jabs in competing statements regarding guidance about immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mayor of America’s biggest city thanks Trump for crackdown on criminal immigrants
item.description: Mayor Eric Adams of New York thanked the Trump administration for cracking down on criminal illegal immigrants, which helped reduce the stream of immigrants in the city by over 1,000%.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Musk tells Cabinet that DOGE email was 'pulse check' for workers, warns US will 'go bankrupt' without action
item.description: Elon Musk said his DOGE productivity email to federal employees was a "pulse check" amid suspicions dead people or "fictional individuals" are on payroll.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate confirms Jamieson Greer to be Trump's top trade negotiator as battles loom
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate has confirmed Jamieson Greer, a veteran of President Donald Trump's first-term economic battles with China, Mexico and Canada, to be America's top trade negotiator. As U.S.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2026 gubernatorial race heats up as DeSantis shifts focus to Casey, Trump backs Donalds
item.description: It may appear to be distant, but the 2026 gubernatorial race is already heating up. President Donald Trump has put his full support behind Congressman Byron Donalds, but Gov. Ron DeSantis does not seem to be on board with that choice.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mayor Adams asks to have charges dropped after leaked letter
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams’ lawyers filed a motion to have his charges dropped after someone “leaked” a letter from now-resigned U.S. Attorney Danielle Sasson, court records show.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: House Democrat introduces bill to reinstate veterans fired from the federal government under Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A freshman Democratic congressman is introducing a bill to protect the jobs of veterans working for the U.S. government amid mass firings by the Trump administration, the latest legislative response to the turmoil rippling across federal agencies. The bill from Rep.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOGE subcommittee looks at US foreign aid spending
item.description: Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene said the hearing comes as Americans are "shocked" to discover how their taxpayer dollars have been spent overseas.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Israel and Hamas agree on a new exchange, leaving a fragile ceasefire intact
item.description: The bodies of four Israeli hostages are to be returned late Wednesday and more than 600 Palestinian prisoners and detainees freed during the last week of the current Gaza ceasefire deal.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin and Hadeel Al-Shalchi, MPR News

item.title: Latvia FM: Putin will try to achieve in peace talks what he couldn't in Ukraine war
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Latvia's foreign minister warns that Russian President Vladimir Putin will try to achieve in peace talks what he hasn’t been able to accomplish in his war against Ukraine — weaken the United States and restore control over his smaller neighbor.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Teachers union sues over Trump administration's deadline to end school diversity programs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A new federal lawsuit in Maryland is challenging a Trump administration memo giving the nation’s schools and universities two weeks to eliminate “race-based” practices of any kind or risk losing their federal money.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Digital license plates allowed under proposal in the Nevada Legislature
item.description: A new bill at the Nevada Legislature would allow the DMV to accept digital license plates purchased from an approved vendor.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: House GOP, after pulling budget resolution, brings it back for a vote
item.description: House GOP leaders brought their budget resolution to the floor for the vote, just minutes after pulling it at the last minute Tuesday evening as they struggled to win over a handful of conservative fiscal hawks who had threatened to tank the framework for President Trump's ambitious legislative agen
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Cicero Primary Election: Incumbent Dominick defeats Rodriguez, will serve 6th straight term
item.description: Incumbent Cicero Town President Larry Dominick will serve a fifth straight four-year term, defeating Esteban Rodriguez in the primary election Tuesday evening.
item.dc:creator: Dana Rebik, WGN 9

item.title: Candidates for NYC mayor work to get on the ballot as Cuomo looms large
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Tuesday was the first day when New York City mayoral candidates could collect signatures to gain access to the primary ballot in June. Former Gov. Andrew Cuomo continues to be favored in the race for mayor, despite not officially declaring.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Federal employees may get more demands to justify their work at Elon Musk's direction
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The turmoil that enveloped the federal workforce over the last few days is unlikely to cease anytime soon as the U.S. government's human resources agency considers how to fulfill Elon Musk ’s demands.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump moves to suspend security clearances of lawyers at DC law firm helping Jack Smith
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday ordered the suspension of any security clearances held by lawyers at a prominent Washington law firm who provided legal services to special counsel Jack Smith, the latest move in the Republican's campaign of retribution over the criminal investigat
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump pitches 'gold card' as replacement for EB5 investor visa
item.description: “We're going to be selling a gold card. You have a green card. This is a gold card. We're going to be putting a price on that card of about $5 million and that's going to give you green card privileges,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Bondi warns states to comply with Trump order on transgender athletes
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi warned officials in Maine, California and Minnesota on Tuesday to comply with President Trump’s executive orders to bar transgender athletes from participating in girls’ and women’s sports, promising swift legal action.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 21 DOGE staffers resign, saying they won’t help ‘dismantle’ public services
item.description: Twenty-one members of the United States DOGE Service have resigned, they said in an anonymous letter, citing DOGE’s ongoing work dramatically reshaping the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court orders new trial for Oklahoma death row inmate
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday sided with an Oklahoma death row inmate who claimed alongside the state itself that his trial was unfair, ordering a new trial and staving off his execution.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Health care workers are rushing to learn about immigration law in case of ICE raids
item.description: A policy change by the Trump administration allows immigration agents to enter and arrest people in health facilities. Some clinics are training health workers to support patients in the event of arrests.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Fortier, MPR News
